justification: Official Rosen senate press release explicitly names both Rosen and Cassidy co-introducing legislation to protect federal data from adversarial AI technologies.
Official Rosen senate press release explicitly names Rosen (HSGAC member) and Portman (Ranking Member) co-introducing the Advancing American AI Innovation Act.
Official Cortez Masto press release welcoming Rosen back, documenting both as Nevada's two senators serving together.
Official McCaul press release explicitly names both McCaul and Under Secretary Helberg discussing Pax Silica and AI supply chain security at a committee hearing.
Official McCaul press release explicitly names both McCaul and Matt Pottinger discussing keeping American AI out of the CCP's hands at an HFAC hearing.
Official House Science Committee press release explicitly names Lofgren and Lucas co-introducing the Workforce for AI Trust Act.
News article documenting Elon Musk bringing in Sriram Krishnan as a technology executive to help revamp Twitter during the 2022 acquisition.
News article on Trump's announcement of Krishnan as Senior Policy Advisor on AI, explicitly stating Krishnan will work closely with David Sacks to shape AI policy.
Official Warner senate press release explicitly names both Warner and Rounds introducing the Economy of the Future Commission Act.
Official Warner senate press release explicitly names both Warner and Hawley leading bipartisan legislation tracking AI job impacts and urging federal agencies to expand data collection.
Official Subramanyam press release explicitly names both Subramanyam and Obernolte introducing the Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act on March 17.
Texas Tribune article reporting on Governor Abbott meeting privately with President Trump at the White House to discuss immigration and border security.
Official California Governor press release explicitly naming Newsom signing Senator Wiener's SB 53 AI transparency bill into law.
Official ACM announcement explicitly naming LeCun, Hinton, and Bengio as the 2018 Turing Award recipients for deep learning contributions.
Official ACM announcement explicitly naming LeCun, Hinton, and Bengio as the 2018 Turing Award recipients for deep learning contributions.
